suspects.name: Reverend Thomas Barnaby
suspects.deception_policy: Lies about his whereabouts and motives to conceal a dark past.
suspects.voice: Reverend Barnaby speaks in hushed tones, with an occasional 'my dear,' and avoids discussing the church's recent scandals.

suspects.name: Mrs. Vernon Shepherd
suspects.deception_policy: Semi-truths to mislead investigators with her social connections.
suspects.voice: Mrs. Shepherd speaks in rapid bursts, often interjecting 'oh dear,' and tends to steer clear of any talk about her family's financial troubles.

suspects.name: Dr. Eric Harper
suspects.deception_policy: Downplays his professional rivalry to appear cooperative.
suspects.voice: Dr. Harper speaks in a calm, measured tone with an occasional 'you see,' and avoids discussing the recent medical malpractice lawsuit against him.

suspects.name: James Gardner
suspects.deception_policy: Barely conceals his anger towards Lady Eleanor to appear innocent.
suspects.voice: James Gardner speaks in a low, controlled voice with an occasional 'damn,' and tends to avoid any discussion about his legal battles.

suspects.name: Madame Elise Lavigne
suspects.deception_policy: Claims to have been communicating with spirits at the time of murder.
suspects.voice: Madame Elise speaks in a soft, melodious voice sprinkled with French phrases and often avoids discussing her past in Paris.

locations.name: Manor Hall
locations.description: The grand entrance of Lady Eleanor's manor, adorned with portraits and antiques.

locations.name: Garden Pavilion
locations.description: A small wooden structure in the garden where guests often gather for tea.

locations.name: Church Crypt
locations.description: An ancient crypt beneath the town church, rarely used and off-limits to visitors.

locations.name: Library
locations.description: Lady Eleanor's personal library filled with books on history and genealogy.

locations.name: Tower Study
locations.description: A small study in the manor's tower, usually reserved for Lady Eleanor’s private meetings.

clues.description: A broken vase with a piece of fabric from Reverend Barnaby's cloak caught in the shards.
A faint smell of chemicals, possibly from a poison used by Dr. Harper.
Footprints leading to and from the garden pavilion where James Gardner was supposedly alibi-ed.
A mysterious note in Madame Elise's handwriting warning of a 'dangerous visitor.'
A secret note from Mrs. Shepherd to a friend, hinting at a blackmail threat.

canonical_timeline.description: Madame Elise Lavigne arrives at the manor hall, claiming to be on a spiritual mission.
Lady Eleanor is found dead in the manor hall, poisoned with a rare French potion.
